title: Optimization of Loop-mediated isothermal amplification assay for detection of Escherichia coli in freshwater
author: Djisalov Mila
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Contamination of water (and food) with fecal bacteria is a well known common and diligent problem affecting public health and 
the local and national economy One of the best indicators of fecal bacterial water pollution is Escherichia coli (E coli) which 
belongs to the group of enteric bacteria and most often creates diarrheal diseases which can have fatal consequences. 
 
Within this study, a comparative analysis using conventional methods (microbiological detection and Real-Time PCR analysis) 
and Loop mediated isothermal amplification.

keywords: LAMP. E. coli, PCR, water contamination
